free_text: Average Neutron Total Cross Sections in the 3- to
12-Mev Region
Main reference.
Preliminary results.
Subsequent
measurement.
Los Alamos fast reactor.
The neutron spectrometer detector is a
parallel plate ionization chamber. A radiator
containing hydrogen or deuterium (polyethylene) is
used to convert the neutrons into proton or deuteron
recoils. List of radiators: 2,6 mg/cm2 of CH2, 7,16,
25 mg/cm2 of CD2. By a combination of proton and
deuteron radiators, a factor of three in neutron
energy can be covered with one gas filling.
The overall accuracy. The statistical
accuracy of the measurements varied from around +-3%
at 3 MeV to about 8% at 13 MeV. The small correction
for inscattering at the higher energies has been
deleted since it is quite small compared to the
statistical error. When all nonstatistical errors are
taken into account the overall accuracy is
approximately +-10%.
Translated from SCISRS.
Converted to reaction formalism.

facility, method, samples, error analysis. Updated

data according to target material.
